problem.statement: Card and wallet payment sessions need one WLT-owned, tenant-isolated and auditable lifecycle from reference creation through authorization, capture, provider uncertainty, webhook confirmation, ledger posting, DSH readback and reconciliation without any double charge or duplicate financial truth.
problem.currentWorkaround: The repository creates WLT payment-session references and guards concurrent authorize/capture calls, but it has no durable operation replay receipts, no signed provider webhook route, no payment capture posting into the double-entry ledger, and no complete payment-session API contract or operator session timeline.

actors.role: Authenticated customer selecting a supported payment method and reading the resulting payment state through DSH.
permittedActions: start one payment session through DSH
retry a recoverable DSH action with the same idempotency identity
forbiddenActions: supply an authorization or capture amount
treat a timeout as success or failure
repeat provider charges from the frontend

actors.role: Authorized financial operations reviewer using the control panel.
permittedActions: inspect provider events and operation receipts
request provider status refresh through a governed service route
forbiddenActions: mark capture complete without provider evidence

actors.role: Authenticated DSH service caller that creates references and reads WLT truth for operational projection.
permittedActions: request authorize capture expiry cancellation and status refresh
forbiddenActions: retry a financial mutation without an idempotency key
change tenant client amount currency or payment method after reference creation

actors.role: Configured external financial provider returning synchronous results and signed asynchronous events.
forbiddenActions: change the bound session amount or currency
reuse one event identity for a different payload

surfaces.exclusionReason: Partner settlement and payout views belong to JRN-036 and JRN-037; partners do not authorize or capture client payment sessions.
Captain cash custody belongs to JRN-038 and must not share the online payment-session mutation path.
Field finance requests and commissions are separate journeys and have no payment-session authorization action.

outcome.statement: Every non-COD payment session is tenant bound, replay safe, provider verifiable, atomically posted to the WLT ledger on capture, projected to DSH without transferring ownership, and recoverable through an auditable reconciliation timeline.
outcome.primaryMetric: captured payment sessions with exactly one matching ledger transaction and DSH readback
guardrailMetrics: captured session without ledger transaction count
ledger posting without captured session count
outcome.baselineState: Session state claims and outbox readback exist, but operation replay, signed webhook intake, capture ledger posting, complete contract binding and operator timeline are incomplete.
outcome.targetState: WLT exposes one governed payment lifecycle with durable idempotency, signed events, balanced ledger posting, DSH projection and recoverable operator readback.
outcome.observationWindow: targeted product, migration, backend, contract, security, finance, runtime, UI and independent review

criteria: Reference creation is service-authenticated, tenant isolated and idempotent for the bound checkout intent and payload.
Authorize and capture require an Idempotency-Key; exact replay never repeats the provider call and payload conflict is rejected.
At most one concurrent caller can claim each authorize or capture transition.
Provider declines become failed while ambiguous transport outcomes become provider_result_unknown with one open reconciliation case.
Signed provider events reject missing, invalid, stale or replay-conflicting signatures and event identities.
A captured transition posts one balanced provider_clearing to platform_payable ledger transaction in the same database transaction.
Captured failed and expired terminal transitions enqueue durable DSH readback without moving financial ownership to DSH.
Provider status refresh resolves unknown results without blindly repeating authorization or capture.
Control panel exposes receipts provider events reconciliation and ledger references through a DSH-authenticated read path.
App client displays canonical pending unknown failed expired and captured states with safe retry and offline recovery.
No frontend or DSH component calls the external financial provider or writes WLT ledger truth.
All required paths are represented in an active OpenAPI contract and typed client/controller binding.
requiredChecks: targeted Go payment and webhook tests
OpenAPI validation and client drift check
runtime provider and DSH readback smoke
Arabic RTL and weak-network visual review

business: WLT is the sole owner of payment provider state and ledger truth.
DSH owns checkout orchestration and stores only WLT references and operational projections.
The amount currency tenant client store and source binding are immutable after payment-session creation.
Every financial mutation carries service authentication tenant context correlation and idempotency.
Every capture state change ledger posting and DSH outbox event commits or rolls back together.
An unknown provider result is reconciled by authoritative status evidence rather than by repeating the financial operation.
negative: No idempotency key represents different payment operation inputs.
No provider webhook is trusted without a configured secret valid signature acceptable timestamp and unique event identity.
No captured session lacks exactly one matching balanced ledger transaction.
No DSH or frontend code writes WLT session ledger reconciliation or provider event tables.
No COD collection uses the online authorization or capture route.
No operator self-approves final financial or product closure.

owners.uxJourneyOwner: Client Checkout and Finance Operations Experience Owner

execution.appetite: Close JRN-034 code, data, contract, security, ledger, readback and surface gaps without moving financial ownership out of WLT, deploying production or self-approving protected evidence.
fixedConstraints: Repository mode is REMOTE_ONLY on sambassam.
WLT remains the financial and provider truth owner.
DSH remains the checkout orchestration and projection owner.
Provider secrets are environment-owned and fail closed.
No PR merge force push release production deployment or commercial SaaS activation is authorized.
Independent product finance security visual release and production approvals cannot be self-issued by engineering.
forbiddenScope: frontend provider credentials or direct provider calls
fake runtime visual or finance evidence
